title: I can't understand why to use a lot of pointer in iopodman.Create.

comments.body:
It makes a distinction between the empty string, "", and completely unset.
A nil means the setting should be left as default, a "" overrides it with
the empty string. The two have very different meanings in some places.

On Sat, Feb 1, 2020, 01:35 shaneing <notifications@github.com> wrote:

> The iopodman.Create as follow:
>
> type Create struct {
>     // ...
>     Entrypoint             *string   `json:"entrypoint,omitempty"`
>     Env                    *[]string `json:"env,omitempty"`
>     // ...
> }
>
> IMHO, the struct is hard to use.
>
> —
> You are receiving this because you are subscribed to this thread.
> Reply to this email directly, view it on GitHub
> <https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/5047?email_source=notifications&email_token=AB3AOCG6W7HIZFAKNZ6O2Z3RAUJZZA5CNFSM4KORJ4FKYY3PNVWWK3TUL52HS4DFUVEXG43VMWVGG33NNVSW45C7NFSM4IKJ63IA>,
> or unsubscribe
> <https://github.com/notifications/unsubscribe-auth/AB3AOCGM7E2WILI6H3YC7ILRAUJZZANCNFSM4KORJ4FA>
> .
>
I have another problem. Why not use `iopodman.ContainerMount` instead of `iopodman.Create.Mount`.

The Varlink API is in the process of being deprecated, so not much we can
do about this right now. Part of the interface for the new HTTP API is
fixed for compatibility, but design of the libpod endpoints is still early
enough that comments would be welcome

On Sat, Feb 1, 2020, 10:42 shaneing <notifications@github.com> wrote:

> I have another problem. Why not use iopodman.ContainerMount instead of
> iopodman.Create.Mount.
>

@mheon ok, thanks. I will follow the HTTP API.
